governs: Prospective safety classification and approval evidence for first-party dependency replacements in the Dependency Sovereignty Program.
The minimum evidence a downstream implementation bead must cite before it can claim that a replacement is safety-eligible.
The fail-closed exception process for replacements that would introduce algorithmic unsafe.
does_not_govern: Whether a replacement is worthwhile on marginal dependency cost, interoperability, performance, maintenance, or product grounds.
Existing unsafe code or existing rows in artifacts/unsafe_boundary_ledger_v1.json.
Third-party dependency safety in general or the safety of transitive dependency implementations.
Release readiness, broad workspace health, runtime correctness, or performance parity.
policy_scope.no_claim_boundary: This taxonomy governs prospective dependency-replacement approvals only. It does not retroactively judge, revoke, certify, or reclassify existing ledgered unsafe boundaries. A matching class row is not proof that its evidence requirements or separate program gates have been satisfied.

eligibility_states.eligible: The safety class permits implementation without an exception when the row and its separate program gates are satisfied.
eligibility_states.eligible_with_required_evidence: The safety class permits implementation only after every class evidence item is linked from the implementation bead.
eligibility_states.prohibited: The replacement must not be implemented or approved under the default policy.
eligibility_states.exception_approved: ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE is permitted only when the complete exception record is present and explicit owner sign-off is linked.

classes.definition: First-party replacement code that is clean under #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and does not rely on first-party unsafe helpers to implement the replacement.
classes.unsafe_scope: No first-party unsafe code is permitted in the replacement surface.
required_evidence: A source or crate-level forbid(unsafe_code) gate, or an equivalent contract test proving the complete replacement surface contains no first-party unsafe code.
Focused unit, differential, and end-to-end evidence required by the owning implementation bead.
UBS coverage for every owned file before approval.
A proof note that names the exact replacement surface and its separate program gates.
review_rules: Do not weaken the class by hiding unsafe behind a new first-party helper or generated file.
Treat retained third-party dependencies and platform libraries as separate trust relationships rather than calling them first-party safe code.
Safety eligibility does not waive semantic parity, deterministic testing, marginal-cost, performance, or interoperability gates.
explicit_no_claims: The absence of first-party unsafe does not prove functional correctness.
SAFE-OWN does not prove performance parity or justify removing the incumbent.
SAFE-OWN does not certify transitive dependencies as safe.

classes.definition: Narrow, ledgered unsafe at an OS/FFI or CPU-dispatch boundary, including syscall wrappers, target-feature dispatch shims, and process-environment mutation.
classes.unsafe_scope: Only the smallest practical boundary item may contain unsafe code; item- or function-scoped #[allow(unsafe_code)] is required when practical.
required_evidence: A written explanation of why a safe-Rust alternative is insufficient for this boundary.
The narrowest practical item- or function-scoped #[allow(unsafe_code)] around the unsafe operation.
A matching row in artifacts/unsafe_boundary_ledger_v1.json with an explicit safety invariant and no-claim boundary.
Miri coverage for executable unsafe behavior, or a documented toolchain or platform limitation plus an alternate focused proof.
UBS coverage for every owned boundary file.
A target-gated RCH proof note that states which hosts or architectures were not exercised.
review_rules: The implementation bead must link every required evidence item; a category label alone is not approval.
Keep OS handles, raw pointers, target-feature assumptions, and ownership transfer inside the ledgered boundary.
Apply any separate measured-defect, portability, marginal-cost, security-review, or owner-signoff gate recorded on the classification row.
explicit_no_claims: Passing the taxonomy contract does not execute or verify platform FFI.
Evidence from one host or architecture does not prove another host or architecture.
A ledger row is an inventory and review contract, not proof that unsafe code is correct.

classes.definition: Unsafe code that encodes an ownership, liveness, initialization, pinning, or weak-memory argument, including lock-free pointer tagging, MaybeUninit inline storage, futex parking protocols, and generated pin projection.
classes.unsafe_scope: Prohibited for dependency replacements by default; no implementation work may start from a prohibited row.
required_evidence: A complete algorithmic_unsafe_exception_process record is mandatory before eligibility can change to exception_approved.
Partial exception evidence fails closed and leaves the row prohibited.
review_rules: Loom does not prove liveness or linearizability.
Miri does not model weak memory.
A 48-hour soak is not a proof.
Moving algorithmic unsafe from a mature, widely-fuzzed crate into fresh first-party code increases risk.
Prefer retaining the incumbent or benchmarking a SAFE-OWN alternative.
explicit_no_claims: Loom, Miri, fuzzing, or soak evidence alone cannot approve an ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE replacement.
A performance hypothesis is not a measured incumbent defect.
Taxonomy contract success does not grant an exception.

requirements.measured_incumbent_performance_defect: A reproducible benchmark or production trace must identify a concrete defect in the incumbent on the real Asupersync workload.
requirements.safe_alternative_benchmark: At least one SAFE-OWN alternative must be implemented far enough to measure on the same workload and full gate axes.
requirements.safe_alternative_rejection_rationale: The decision record must explain why the measured SAFE-OWN result is unacceptable and why no narrower safe design satisfies the requirement.
requirements.owner_signoff: The repository owner must explicitly approve the named algorithmic-unsafe replacement after reviewing the defect and safe-alternative evidence.

no_implicit_approval: An open bead, prototype, benchmark plan, or taxonomy classification is not owner sign-off.
Evidence for a mature incumbent cannot be reused as evidence for fresh first-party unsafe code.
No field may be omitted, marked pending, or inferred from another field.

classifications.replacement_surface: Scalar hexadecimal encode and decode helpers.
program_gates: Differential vectors against the retained incumbent.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: Triviality, not graph weight, is the replacement justification.

classifications.replacement_surface: Scalar base64 engines for WebSocket, certificate pin, gRPC-Web, and NATS credential surfaces.
program_gates: Differential vectors for every accepted alphabet and padding policy.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: The replacement must not reintroduce an unsafe SIMD backend.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned block_on, poll_fn, zip, race, and yield_now test and utility helpers.
program_gates: Differential behavior against futures-lite as a dev-only oracle.
No second production executor or orphan-task path.
classifications.notes: The implementation must preserve deterministic test behavior and structured concurrency.

classifications.replacement_surface: Generation-tagged in-tree TokenSlab family.
program_gates: ABA and stale-token regression coverage.
Differential allocation and removal behavior.
classifications.notes: Generation tagging is required; an unsafe MaybeUninit slab would be a different class.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned asupersync-macros visibility attribute.
program_gates: Compile-pass and compile-fail macro fixtures.
Expansion parity for every existing use shape.
classifications.notes: The attribute expansion may not emit unsafe code.

classifications.replacement_surface: Nanosecond timestamp to RFC3339 formatter.
program_gates: Boundary, leap-year, and golden-format vectors.
Behavioral parity at the sole production call site.
classifications.notes: This row covers the owned formatter, not a general time library.

classifications.replacement_surface: Explicit purpose-built snapshot and trace schemas replacing public Bincode format variants.
program_gates: Phase-3 public API ADR approval.
Versioned golden and differential corpus.
Migration and compatibility policy for stored artifacts.
classifications.notes: Eligibility does not pre-approve the public API redesign.

classifications.replacement_surface: Explicit purpose-built typed-symbol, snapshot, and trace serialization replacing public MessagePack format variants.
program_gates: Phase-3 public API ADR approval.
Versioned golden and differential corpus.
Shared-file reservation coordination with the bincode surface.
classifications.notes: Bincode and MessagePack work share typed_symbol.rs and must not be executed as conflicting parallel edits.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned versioned configuration schemas and migration logic over retained serde_json.
program_gates: Owner-approved TOML and YAML to versioned JSON migration ADR.
Data-preservation inventory, semantic goldens, and manual corpus review.
Explicit owner permission before deleting any legacy file.
classifications.notes: This row does not approve a home-grown TOML or YAML parser.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned parser for the complete documented Asupersync CLI contract.
program_gates: Phase-3 CLI API ADR approval.
OsString and invalid-UTF-8 coverage, help and error goldens, and exit-code parity.
Full capability inventory across every clap-consuming file.
classifications.notes: SAFE-OWN does not reduce the XL implementation scope or permit a partial parser.

classifications.replacement_surface: Fixed PII scanners and an owned subset matcher.
program_gates: Unsupported syntax fails closed.
Differential corpus covers every currently accepted pattern.
PII redaction must never silently degrade.
classifications.notes: The production mod-regex mock is not an acceptable replacement.

classifications.replacement_surface: NKey base32 no-pad, CRC-16/XMODEM, and prefix codec over retained ed25519-dalek.
program_gates: Security review before cutover.
NATS fixture and differential vectors against nkeys as a dev-only oracle.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: The codec replacement does not replace or reimplement ed25519-dalek.

classifications.replacement_surface: Finite owned ProtoMessage types and protobuf wire codec.
program_gates: Phase-3 ProstCodec public API ADR approval.
Differential proptest coverage against prost as a dev-only oracle.
OTLP and gRPC golden vectors for the finite message inventory.
classifications.notes: The class does not approve retaining an arbitrary prost::Message public surface.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned exporter traits and native OTLP export over the finite owned protobuf surface.
program_gates: Explicit ADR approval to remove external OpenTelemetry Meter and SDK interoperability.
Owned protobuf prerequisite complete.
Wire-format and collector interoperability evidence.
classifications.notes: Safety eligibility does not approve the interoperability reduction.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned UtcTimestamp, RFC3339, and serde implementations.
program_gates: Complete call-site inventory.
Differential timestamp, range, and serialization vectors.
classifications.notes: The scope is the finite current timestamp surface, not a general calendar library.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned sync/oslock.rs wrapper over std::sync Mutex, RwLock, and Condvar with owned guard types.
program_gates: Stable lane remains supported.
Full-axis performance gate across 1, 8, 32, and 64 cores.
Optional nightly nonpoison backend must remain SAFE-OWN and feature-gated.
Owner acceptance for every measured regression.
classifications.notes: A future raw futex or parking backend is not covered by this SAFE-OWN row and is classified separately as ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned finite LZ4 block codec.
program_gates: Differential round-trip and malformed-input corpus.
Performance and compression-ratio gate.
classifications.notes: Any later SIMD acceleration must cite the separate simd-dispatch-boundary row.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned finite DEFLATE codec for the retained interoperability surface.
program_gates: Format interoperability and malformed-input corpus.
Performance, ratio, and memory-use gate.
Explicit scope decision for compression capabilities.
classifications.notes: This stretch row remains SAFE-OWN; unsafe acceleration requires a different classification.

classifications.replacement_surface: Cross-platform reactor boundary covering epoll, kqueue, IOCP, wakeups, fd reuse, generation tokens, and registration races.
program_gates: Measured defect or suite-wide platform-boundary project.
Cross-platform conformance evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The current marginal cost is about one package; taxonomy eligibility is not a recommendation to replace polling.

classifications.replacement_surface: Cross-platform socket creation, SockAddr conversion, abstract Unix addresses, SockRef, keepalive, and option handling.
program_gates: Measured defect or suite-wide platform-boundary project.
Cross-platform socket conformance evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The current marginal cost is about one package; taxonomy eligibility is not a recommendation to replace socket2.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned cross-platform signal registration and process-signal boundary.
program_gates: Measured performance, maintenance, or idiom-unification justification.
Cross-platform process lifecycle evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: signal-hook compiles no C in the current feature set; native-code removal is not a valid standalone claim.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned host and process resource probes over procfs, sysctl, and Windows APIs.
program_gates: Measured generality, performance, or maintenance defect.
Resource-monitor hot-path performance gate.
Cross-platform focused evidence and explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The signal kill path must be coordinated with signal-platform rather than duplicated.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned Unix extended-attribute boundary preserving dereference and no-dereference semantics plus macOS signatures.
program_gates: Final-rustix-parent trigger or a broader platform-boundary project.
Per-platform xattr round-trip evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: Adding unsafe solely to remove one small pure-Rust crate is not approved by this row.

classifications.replacement_surface: Strict DER residue for SPKI extraction and SAN access that cannot be delegated to rustls or webpki.
program_gates: Delegate-to-webpki inventory complete.
Differential fuzz corpus and independent security review.
Full-input consumption, DER canonicality, depth, duplicate-extension, and fail-closed ambiguity proofs.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: This row never approves first-party certificate chain validation, name constraints, wildcards, or path building.

classifications.replacement_surface: Target-feature dispatch shims for an otherwise safe owned codec or numerical kernel.
program_gates: Safe scalar implementation exists and remains the parity oracle.
Architecture-specific benchmark demonstrates the need for unsafe acceleration.
Codegen and target-feature gate review.
classifications.notes: The boundary class applies only to dispatch and intrinsics, not to unsafe ownership or initialization tricks inside the kernel.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party lock-free queue using pointer tagging, epoch ownership, or manual reclamation.
program_gates: Benchmark a SAFE-OWN mutex-backed alternative first.
Retain the incumbent unless the complete exception process is approved.
classifications.notes: The incumbent's mature, widely-fuzzed algorithmic unsafe is part of its value.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party inline storage using MaybeUninit or manual drop and initialization tracking.
program_gates: Retain smallvec under the default policy.
Do not treat a future nonzero marginal as safety approval.
classifications.notes: Marginal dependency savings cannot change the safety class.

classifications.replacement_surface: Generated first-party pin-projection machinery.
program_gates: Retain pin-project under the default policy.
A suite-wide macro consolidation still requires the complete exception process.
classifications.notes: The small marginal reduction does not justify fresh soundness-critical macro code.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party futex, WaitOnAddress, or os_unfair_lock parking protocol behind sync/oslock.rs.
program_gates: The SAFE-OWN std::sync wrapper must be implemented and benchmarked first.
Retain the safe backend unless the complete exception process is approved.
classifications.notes: The optional nightly nonpoison backend in parking-lot-wrapper is not this raw-lock fallback and must remain safe.

explicit_no_claims: This citation does not prove marginal dependency savings or broad workspace health.

validation.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${RCH_TARGET_BASE:-${TMPDIR:-/tmp}}/rch_target_dependency_safety_taxonomy" cargo test -p asupersync --test dependency_safety_taxonomy_contract -- --nocapture
